rules: Use aligned optional extension only when it deepens the same lesson thinking.
Keep extension prompts visually secondary and clearly optional.
Do not let early-finisher space start a new task or a different lesson.

anti_sameness_rules: Do not restate artifact purpose when headings and layout already make it obvious.
Reduce repeated helper text that explains the page too much.
Prefer one short aligned extension over stacked support reminders plus filler.
Allow consistency, but avoid cloned phrasing across days or artifact roles.
student_facing_voice_rules: Use shorter, more direct classroom language in student-facing fields.
Prefer spoken phrasing over polished instructional-product wording.
Trust layout and task structure so the prose can do less.
Avoid repeated compliance language and artifact-management reminders unless they are instructionally necessary.
When a task is concrete, use concrete verbs and nouns instead of abstract framing.
model_language_realism: Student-facing models should sound plausible, not perfected.
Avoid hyper-polished, morally over-finished, or unnaturally self-aware model language.
Keep model structure clear without turning it into an idealized exemplar.
